title: Fitting a bi-fold wardrobe door kit
subtitle: Hang a pair of folding doors on a proprietary track to close off a wardrobe opening
excerpt: How to fit a bi-fold door kit to a standard wardrobe or alcove opening. Covers fixing the top track, fitting the pivot pins, hanging the door panels, and adjusting the top guide until the doors close flat and fold cleanly.
sourceNotes: Häfele hardware fitting guides (publicly available). JELD-WEN and similar bi-fold door installation instructions (referenced format, not reproduced). Collins Complete DIY Manual, Albert Jackson and David Day (1989).

glossaryTerms.definition: The top and bottom pivot points of a bi-fold door system. The bottom pivot pin fits into a floor plate and carries the weight of the door panel; the top pivot fits into the top track and provides the hinge point about which the door folds. The top pin has lateral adjustment; the bottom pin has height adjustment. Both must be set precisely for the door to fold without binding.

A nylon roller that travels in the top track to keep the leading edge of the door aligned with the track. Without the guide, the door would swing outward at the top when opened. Adjusted by raising or lowering the guide pin until the door hangs vertical in the closed position.

content.text: A bi-fold door kit consists of two door panels joined by a centre hinge, top and bottom

, and a track. The kit installs in a standard door opening: the track fixes to the head of the opening; the pivot pins fix to the floor and the track at the opening sides. Most proprietary kits include all hardware, but the doors themselves are purchased separately to match the opening dimensions. Measure the opening precisely before ordering.

A bi-fold door kit with track, pivot brackets, and guide. Door panels (pair) to suit the opening; typically MDF, solid timber, or louvre panels. Cordless drill with HSS and masonry bits. Spirit level. Tape measure. A screwdriver set. Masonry fixings if fixing to a plaster-and-lath or masonry head.
Step 1, measure and prepare the opening

Measure the clear opening width at three points (top, middle, and bottom) and use the smallest measurement. Measure the clear height. The door panels should be ordered to the opening width and height less the clearances specified in the kit instructions (typically 10 mm at the top and 5 mm at the bottom). Check the opening for plumb and level: a door hung in an out-of-square opening will not close flat.

Fix the top track to the head of the opening using the screws provided. Check it is level with the spirit level. The pivot bracket at one end of the track fits in a fixed position; the guide channel runs the full length. On a timber-framed head, screw directly into the timber. On a masonry or concrete head, use the SDS drill to make pilot holes and fix with appropriate masonry screws or plugs.
Step 3, fix the floor pivot plate

The bottom pivot plate fixes to the floor directly below the top pivot bracket position. Use the spirit level to transfer the top pivot position down to the floor and mark it. Fix the floor plate with the screws provided. The bottom pivot pin screws into the door panel stile; fit it before hanging the door. Height is adjustable: set it to give the 5 mm floor clearance specified in the kit instructions.
Step 4, hang and adjust the doors

Hinge the two door panels together at their centre edge using the centre hinge included in the kit. Insert the top pivot pin into the track bracket. Drop the bottom pivot pin into the floor plate. Fit the top guide roller to the leading stile of the front panel and slide it into the track. Test the fold action: the door should fold and unfold smoothly without binding. Adjust the top pivot laterally and the bottom pivot vertically until the door panels are plumb and the gap between the closed doors and the opening edges is even. Fit the stop bead and handle according to the kit instructions.

attrs.symptom: Door binding or sticking when folding
attrs.cause: Top guide not adjusted to allow the door to travel freely in the track.
attrs.fix: Loosen the top guide pin, adjust the roller position, and retighten. Check that the track is level and that the pivot pin clearances are correct.

attrs.symptom: Doors not closing flush — a gap at one side in the closed position
attrs.cause: Top pivot bracket not positioned directly above the floor pivot plate.
attrs.fix: Loosen the top pivot bracket, shift it laterally the necessary distance, retighten, and re-test the closed position.
